search_space_rows.reading: Search stays on a discrete grid so that policy learning remains auditable and does not hide behind tiny continuous adjustments.
Adds are only allowed to lift an existing position from a lower valid band to a higher valid band.
Reduce learning is explicitly non-binary. The search should learn trims before it learns exits.
Hard veto remains outside the learning search. The protocol searches soft expression, not whether to ignore invalid states.

reward_rows.reading: The policy should be rewarded for increasing size when future continuation actually pays.
Strong-board days with weak gross exposure must be penalized directly; otherwise the learner will rediscover timidity.
Drawdown still matters, but as one term among several rather than the only thing the system fears.
This prevents the learner from gaming the reward through noisy add/reduce churn.
The search should not earn freedom by breaking already-validated hard invalid-state rules.
This captures the exact failure the replay exposed: staying too light even when the board and source semantics already justify size.

validation_rows.reading: Search parameters must be fitted on earlier windows and scored on later windows only.
The protocol must not mix the same board cycle across train and test in order to avoid path memorization.
This search is not allowed to rewrite stock selection or hard-veto logic while tuning position expression.
Promotion should require better expression efficiency without surrendering the current drawdown discipline.
Every learned action must be reconstructible as state -> action-band -> target-weight, not just as an opaque score.

interpretation: V1.13Z turns the current sizing discussion into a concrete search protocol instead of letting the project drift into unconstrained random exploration.
The learner is allowed to search expression bands and add/reduce moves, but it is not allowed to rewrite validated selection or hard-veto semantics.
This preserves the current research assets while opening a legitimate path toward self-improving sizing behaviour.
